commands.repair_reason: Maintainers need to decide the safe native allowlist contract before automation can repair this PR, and the contributor still needs real behavior proof.; Needs attention: The diff changes a security-sensitive tool allowlist boundary and can widen a restricted native-tool list into full Codex native code mode.; Review finding: [P1] Preserve restricted native allowlists — extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.ts... commands.review_summary:
The PR changes Codex app-server tool-surface gating so any explicit `toolsAllow` entry matching a Codex-native tool enables native code mode, and adds unit coverage for native and non-native names.

PR surface: Source +7, Tests +29. Total +36 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source: current main filters native names out of dynamic tools and only enables native code mode for undefined or wildcard `toolsAllow`, so an explicit native list loses both surfaces. I did not run a live cron because this review is read-only.
commands.review_followup: Maintainers need to decide the safe native allowlist contract before automation can repair this PR, and the contributor still needs real behavior proof.

target.title: fix(codex): enable native tool surface when toolsAllow lists native tools
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes silent failure when `toolsAllow` explicitly lists native Codex tools (read/write/edit/exec).

## Problem

When `toolsAllow` is set to an explicit list like `["read", "write", "edit"]`, `shouldEnableCodexAppServerNativeToolSurface` returns `false` because:
1. Native tools are excluded from dynamic tools (`CODEX_APP_SERVER_OWNED_DYNAMIC_TOOL_EXCLUDES`)
2. Native tool surface is only enabled for wildcard (`*`) or undefined `toolsAllow`

Result: agent reports "no write tools" but `lastRunStatus: ok` — silent data loss for memory-consolidation crons and other restricted-tool workflows.

## Fix

Check if `toolsAllow` contains any native tool names and enable the native tool surface accordingly.

| File | Changes |
|------|---------|
| `extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.ts` | +10 | Add native tool detection to `shouldEnableCodexAppServerNativeToolSurface` |
| `extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts` | +27 | 8 test cases for native/non-native toolsAllow |

Maintains fail-closed behavior for non-native tools (`message`, `web_search`).

Fixes: #86423
target.author: linux2010
